_strict_runtime_reconciliation.reason: candidate human-reviewed Stage B strict runtime baseline reconciliation

ideal_must_detect.matched_text: removing non-essential health declaration and emergency contact fields

must_detect.reason: promoted from strict runtime finding during Stage B baseline reconciliation

must_detect.matched_text:
Ms. Amira Ko on 27 May 2026 via privacy@wattleriver.au; access was provided within 3 business days in line with APP 12.
Mr. Declan Shaw withdrew consent on 25 May 2026 for pre-employment credit screening; processing was halted and the credit bureau request cancelled.
Erasure was completed for Mr. Shaw’s screening file and three other affected applicants on 27 May 2026, with CloudHarbour deletion certificate CH-DEL-5521 attached.
Field mapping was corrected to minimise collection by removing non-essential health declaration and emergency contact fields from the export job.
Retention controls were re-applied per Records Schedule RS-AU-EMP-04, and the exported dataset was truncated to 30 days with automatic purge enabled.
We assessed whether the export included any not generally available information likely to affect our ASX-listed securities; only anonymised headcount snapshots already disclosed in our May operations update were present.
The Board-approved Insider Information Standard requires pre-clearance before sharing price-sensitive ESG metrics, but no such metrics were in the dataset.
There is no evidence of unauthorised access or exfiltration beyond the approved SG tenant; audit trails reconcile to service account SRV-SUP-APAC-17.
ASIC notification was considered and not required as harm likelihood was low and containment complete; internal CAPA CAP-2026-19 is closed.
For queries, contact Ms. Rina Caldwell, Privacy Officer, rina.caldwell@wattleriver.au
must_detect.reason: promoted from strict runtime finding during Stage B baseline reconciliation

uncertain.reason: DSAR handling event is legally relevant but there is no explicit DSAR rule in the allowed set.
Deletion/erasure completion is a key privacy event but there is no erasure rule in the allowed set.
Retention control language is legally relevant but not mapped to an allowed rule.
Specific retention period is relevant but not covered by an allowed rule.
Automated deletion control is relevant but lacks a mapped rule.
A deletion certificate evidences erasure but is not covered by an allowed rule.
Relates to insider-information governance but not an explicit allowed MNPI marker like insider list or information barrier.
Potentially identifying internal account string but not clearly mapped to any allowed PII identifier rule.
